items.question: What does the Subject Consistency AI workflow do?
items.answer: This workflow helps you keep the same subject—like a character, product, or mascot—looking consistent across many generated images. It is designed as a subject consistency generator so your visuals stay on-model even as you change scenes, poses, or angles.

items.question: What kind of reference image should I upload for best consistency?
items.answer: Use a clear, well-lit image that shows the subject's key features: face, proportions, colors, and distinctive details. Avoid blurry or heavily cropped references. A strong anchor image makes it easier for the workflow to preserve identity across new generations.

items.question: Can I generate the same character in different poses and scenes?
items.answer: Yes. After you upload a subject reference, you can describe new poses, actions, and environments in your prompt. The workflow focuses on keeping the core identity and visual details consistent while changing context around the subject.

items.question: How is this different from style consistency?
items.answer: Subject consistency is about keeping who or what appears in the image recognizable—characters, products, or mascots. Style consistency focuses on harmonizing the overall visual style, such as line quality, color treatment, and rendering. You can combine both in your broader workflow, but this template emphasizes subjects staying on-model.

items.question: What are typical use cases for consistent subject generation?
items.answer: Teams use this workflow to maintain brand mascots across campaigns, keep product shots aligned across many scenes, or ensure recurring characters look the same in marketing, documentation, and UI illustrations.

items.question: How do credits and costs work for Subject Consistency generations?
items.answer: Each generation consumes credits based on the model settings. A good practice is to first validate that your reference image and prompts give you the desired level of consistency, then generate larger sets once you are confident in the setup.
